Intro: First 1 Second Matters
Press enter or click to view image in full size

You did not plan to write an if-else jungle.
It happened quietly.
One condition.
Then another.
Then a feature request landed late on Friday.
Now the file scares you.
You scroll slowly.
You hesitate before touching anything.
You know one wrong change will break three flows you barely remember.
This is not bad coding.
This is normal Angular growth.
But senior developers do not fight this with better if-else blocks.
They replace the pattern entirely.
And once you see it, you never go back.
The Real Problem With If-Else Chains
If-else logic fails for one simple reason.
It centralizes decisions in places that should only coordinate behavior.
What usually happens
run(type: string) {
if (type === 'a') {
return this.a.run();
}if (type === 'b') {
return this.b.run();
}
